Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code to address the Supreme Court of Canada decision in R. v. Jordan
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Criminal Code to address the Supreme Court of Canada decision in R. v. Jordan

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to codify the analytical framework the Supreme Court of Canada set out in its decision in R. v. Jordan regarding the right of an accused awaiting trial before a superior or provincial court to be tried within a reasonable time, and to provide for exceptions to this framework.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: The Criminal Code is amended by adding the following after section 529.5:

Heading.TitleText: Reasonable Time for Trial Before Superior or Provincial Court

Section.MarginalNote: Ceiling â trial before superior court

Subsection.Text: A superior court judge must stay proceedings if the period between the day on which a charge is laid and the actual or expected end of trial before the court exceeds 30 months, unless the prosecutor establishes that the period is reasonable by demonstrating that exceptional circumstances exist.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Ceiling â trial before provincial court
Subsection.Text: A provincial court judge must stay proceedings if the period between the day on which a charge is laid and the actual or expected end of trial before the court exceeds 18 months, unless the prosecutor establishes that the period is reasonable by demonstrating that exceptional circumstances exist.

Any delay that is caused solely or directly by the defenceâs conduct or that is waived by the defence does not count in calculating whether the ceiling established in subsection (1) or (2) has been reached.
Subsections (1) and (2) do not apply in respect of a primary designated offence, as defined in section 487.04.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms
Subsection.Text: In accordance with subsection 33(1) of the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms, it is declared that subsection (4) operates despite paragraph 11(b) of the Canadian Charter of Rights and Freedoms.
